layers.name: App Routes
layers.description: Next.js App Router root layouts, global styles, EngiWeb studio pages (home, services), and landing-page shell components.

layers.name: Company Site
layers.description: ARGOSDYNE company clone: corporate routes, company components, MainNavbar/MainFooter, and company content types/data.

layers.name: Demo Sites
layers.description: Industry showcase demos under app/demos, demo-specific components, demo data modules, and per-demo TypeScript types.

layers.name: Shared UI
layers.description: Reusable UI primitives (buttons, headings), theme provider/toggle used across EngiWeb and demos.

layers.name: Data & Content
layers.description: Static content modules for the EngiWeb marketing site (industries, services, process, demo gallery metadata).

layers.name: Shared Libraries
layers.description: Cross-cutting utilities: theme class tokens, helpers, demo icons, and root shared types.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project docs, AI generation rules, design system guides, and README/AGENTS files.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Build tooling and framework config: package.json, TypeScript, ESLint, Next.js, PostCSS.

tour.title: Welcome to EngiWeb
tour.description: EngiWeb is a Next.js 16 showcase repo with two distinct surfaces: the ARGOSDYNE company site at `/` and a gallery of industry demo websites under `/demos`. Start with README.md for setup, then AGENTS.md — the AI entry point that points every contributor to the docs/ folder. docs/PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md is the map of the whole codebase: where pages, components, data, and types live.

tour.title: Rules & Conventions
tour.description: Before writing code, read the docs/ rule files. docs/AI_GENERATION_RULES.md is the primary guide for AI assistants; docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md defines colors, typography, and component patterns. Together they establish the non-negotiables: use `tc` from lib/theme-classes.ts, keep content in lib/data/, and never redesign existing visual styles.

tour.title: Root Layout & Global Theme
tour.description: Every page flows through app/layout.tsx (RootLayout). It loads Google fonts, wraps children in ThemeProvider (next-themes), and imports app/globals.css where Tailwind v4 design tokens and utility classes are defined. This is the single place that establishes light/dark mode and base typography for the entire app.

tour.title: ARGOSDYNE Homepage
tour.description: The root route `/` is the ARGOSDYNE drone company site, not the EngiWeb marketing page. app/page.tsx composes company-specific sections from components/company/HomeSections.tsx (hero, product/solution/support previews, partners, news) and closes with CTASection. Page files stay thin — they orchestrate sections, not inline content.

tour.title: Company Shell & Content Data
tour.description: The ARGOSDYNE site shares chrome via MainNavbar and MainFooter, both wired to lib/data/companyContent.ts for nav links, hero slides, products, solutions, and support items. All repeated copy lives in this data module — components import arrays and helpers like getProductBySlug rather than hardcoding JSX strings.

tour.title: Product, Solution & Support Routes
tour.description: ARGOSDYNE inner pages follow a consistent pattern: thin app/ route files delegate to rich content components. `/product` and `/product/[slug]` use ProductPageContent and ProductDetailContent; `/solution` uses SolutionPageContent; `/support` uses SupportPageContent. Each content component pulls from companyContent.ts and reuses shared cards and CTAs.

tour.title: Demo Sites Gallery
tour.description: Switch context to EngiWeb's demo showcase at `/demos`. app/demos/page.tsx renders a card grid driven by the demos array in lib/data/demos.ts — the single registry of active demo sites (autonomy-tech, construction-engineering, biotech-research, industrial-product). Adding a new gallery demo means registering it here first.

tour.title: Design System & Theme Classes
tour.description: Styling is centralized, not ad hoc. lib/theme-classes.ts exports the `tc` object — theme-aware Tailwind class strings for headings, body text, buttons, and cards. app/globals.css holds CSS-first design tokens (colors, surfaces, glow effects). docs/THEME_RULES.md and docs/DESIGN_SYSTEM.md document when and how to use each token.

tour.title: Demo Pattern: Industrial Product Setup
tour.description: Every multi-page demo follows the same skeleton. industrial-product uses app/demos/industrial-product/layout.tsx for shared navbar/footer (IndustrialDemoNavbar, IndustrialDemoFooter) and lib/data/industrial-product/site.ts for route constants — never hardcode `/demos/industrial-product/...` paths in JSX. This layout + site.ts + data folder trio is the template for all demos.

tour.title: Demo Deep Dive: Homepage Composition
tour.description: The industrial-product homepage (app/demos/industrial-product/page.tsx) switches between drone and phone product modes, composing DroneHero and DroneHomeSections from components/demos/industrial-product/. Section content comes from lib/data/industrial-product/drone-data.ts and related data files — the page is a composer, not a content dump.

tour.title: Demo Deep Dive: Inner Product Pages
tour.description: Inner demo routes extend the same pattern. `/demos/industrial-product/products` lists product cards; individual product pages compose AeroX2ProductPage with specialized sub-components (DroneProductShowcase, DroneComparisonTable, DroneCTA). Follow this route → thin page → section components → lib/data/ flow when building or modifying any demo inner page.
